questions.question: Which of the following organisms contains Chlorophyll in its cells?
questions.explanation: Euglena contains chlorophyll and can perform photosynthesis, unlike the other organisms listed.

questions.question: Species are groups of organisms that

options.A: are of different origins and are always found together
options.B: are of common origin but cannot interbreed freely
options.C: have similar features and can interbreed freely
options.D: resemble each other and occupy different niches

questions.explanation: Species are groups of organisms that have similar features and can interbreed to produce fertile offspring.

questions.question: A plant with short, vertical underground stem is a
questions.explanation: A corm is a short, vertical, underground stem that stores nutrients.

questions.question: The diagram above is an illustration of a reproductive process in an organism. Study it and answer questions 4 and 5.
questions.explanation: The diagram depicts binary fission, a form of asexual reproduction common in unicellular organisms.

questions.question: The illustrated organism is capable of reproducing by
questions.explanation: Many organisms can reproduce both sexually and asexually, depending on conditions.

questions.question: Which of the following organisms consists of unicellular cells joined to form a filament?
questions.explanation: Spirogyra is a filamentous green alga made up of unicellular cells joined in a chain.

questions.question: The structure in the cell that controls the movement of substances in and out of the cell is the
questions.explanation: The cytoplasmic (cell) membrane regulates substances entering and leaving the cell.

questions.question: The bursting of a red blood cell placed in a hypotonic solution is called
questions.explanation: Haemolysis is the rupture of red blood cells due to osmotic imbalance in hypotonic solutions.

questions.question: The function of the root hair in plants is to

options.A: absorb soil water by the process of osmosis
options.B: excrete water and mineral salts by simple diffusion
options.C: purify the soil for the plant

questions.explanation: Root hairs increase the surface area for water absorption from soil.

questions.question: If a germinating seed is attached horizontally to a revolving klinostat, what would be the effect on the seedling after three days?

options.A: plumule will curve vertically upward while the radicle will not show any curvature
options.B: radicle will curve towards the ground while the plumule will not show any curvature
options.C: plumule and radicle will not show any curvature
options.D: plumule will curve vertically upwards while the radicle will curve towards the ground

questions.explanation: The klinostat causes the plant to grow with a tendency to curve away from gravity, resulting in upward and downward curvature.

questions.question: The lumbar vertebra differs from the thoracic vertebra because it possesses a
questions.explanation: Lumbar vertebrae have thicker, larger centra to support more weight.

questions.question: Which of the following blood vessels carries oxygenated blood into the heart?
questions.explanation: Pulmonary veins carry oxygenated blood from the lungs to the heart.

questions.question: Filtrates in the Bowman's capsule contain vitamins because

options.B: most of them are fat soluble
options.C: they cannot be reabsorbed into the blood
options.D: little amount is required by the blood

questions.explanation: Vitamins are small molecules with low molecular weight, allowing them to be filtered into the capsule.

questions.question: The structures that contain grey matter and white matter are found in the
questions.explanation: Grey and white matter are found in the brain and spinal cord, parts of the nervous system.

questions.question: The diagram above is an illustration of sense organ in humans. Study it and answer questions 15 and 16.
questions.explanation: The labelled part I is the protective outer layer, which is the sclera.

questions.question: The part labelled III is the
questions.explanation: Part III is the pupil, the opening in the iris controlling light entry.

questions.question: The diagram above is an illustration of two plants P and Q. Arrows I, II and III illustrate the transfer of pollen grains among flowers of plants P and Q. Study it and answer questions 17 and 18.
questions.explanation: The arrows indicate types of pollination: self-pollination (I), cross-pollination (II and III).

questions.question: The condition necessary for reproduction to occur between plants P and Q after pollination is that plants P and Q should be
questions.explanation: Reproduction typically occurs between plants of the same species.

questions.question: The diagram above illustrates a dry dehiscent fruit. Use it to answer questions 19 and 20.
questions.explanation: Many dry dehiscent fruits disperse seeds explosively.

questions.question: The Parachute-like tuft of hairs developed from the
questions.explanation: The tuft of hairs aiding dispersal is derived from the pedicel.

questions.question: Which of the following events does not occur in the light reaction stage of photosynthesis?
questions.explanation: Carbon dioxide does not participate in the light-dependent reactions; it is involved in the Calvin cycle.

questions.question: The rate of photosynthesis decreases in a wilted herbaceous plant because the

options.A: enzymes in the leaf were denatured
options.B: chlorophyll in the leaves was not broken down
options.C: amount of light reaching the plant was reduced
options.D: amount of carbon dioxide entering the leaf decrease

questions.explanation: Wilted plants have damaged enzymes affecting photosynthesis.

questions.question: The element required for chlorophyll formation in plants is
questions.explanation: Iron is a key component of chlorophyll molecules.

questions.question: The process of food digestion in mammals would

options.A: make energy immediately available to the body
options.B: promote growth and repair of tissues
options.C: provide sufficient food materials within the body
options.D: render food substances soluble and absorbable

questions.explanation: Digestion breaks down food into soluble forms that can be absorbed.

questions.question: Which of the following food substances would give a negative reaction with Benedict's reagent?
questions.explanation: Sucrose does not react with Benedict's reagent unless broken down into monosaccharides.

questions.question: Sunlight is the major source of
questions.explanation: Sunlight catalyzes the production of Vitamin D in human skin.

questions.question: Discouraging the use of poison to kill fish in a large water body is a way of

options.C: allowing the fittest organism to survive
options.D: making the fish adapt to their environment

questions.explanation: Using poison harms the ecosystem; discouraging it helps conserve fish populations.

questions.question: The pathway through which nutrients are replenished and circulated in an ecosystem is called a
questions.explanation: Nutrient cycles (like the nitrogen or carbon cycle) circulate essential elements.

questions.question:
The illustration below is a food chain. Study it and answer questions 29 and 30. 

             Grasses → Grasshoppers → Lizards → Hawks

options.A: the grasses eaten by grasshoppers is greater than the number of grasshoppers
options.B: the grasses eaten by grasshoppers is fewer than the number of grasshoppers
options.C: grasshoppers eaten by lizards is greater than the number of lizards
options.D: lizards eaten by hawks is greater than the number of hawks

questions.explanation: In a food chain, the number of prey (grass) is typically greater than the number of predators (grasshoppers).

questions.question: If the individual species of organisms in the food chain are counted and arranged as a pyramid, the result would be a pyramid of
questions.explanation: Number pyramids represent the count of individuals at each level.

questions.question: Which of the following gases is released during the decomposition of organic matter?
questions.explanation: Hydrogen sulphide is a gas released during the decay of organic matter containing sulfur.

questions.question: The diagrams above are illustrations of organisms from different habitats. Study them and answer questions 32 and 33.
questions.explanation: The labelled structures are likely for chemical defence mechanisms.

questions.question: Habitats of the organisms respectively are
questions.explanation: Based on the images, the habitats are pond and leaf litter.

questions.question: An environmental problem caused by Sulphur (IV) oxide is
questions.explanation: Sulphur oxides react with water vapor to form acid rain.

questions.question: The phenomenon by which a farmland that is left to fallow for several years develops into a forest is called
questions.explanation: Ecological succession describes natural changes in ecosystems over time.

questions.question: The plants shown above are germinated on a small piece of land. Which of the following effects would not be observed?
questions.explanation: Mutualism is unlikely among identical plants competing for resources.

questions.question: The bar chart above shows data of sources of energy used for cooking. The source most used in the community is
questions.explanation: Kerosene appears to be the most used energy source in the chart.

questions.question: The two sources of energy that would mostly lead to pollution in the community are
questions.explanation: Coal combustion and kerosene use produce significant pollutants.

questions.question: An individual found a strange bird tagged as an endangered species. What should the individual do?

options.B: Take it to an aquatic environment
options.C: Return it to its natural habitat
options.D: Sell it at a very high price

questions.explanation: Endangered species should be protected and returned to their habitat.

questions.question: Which of the following traits is not inheritable? Ability to
questions.explanation: Taste P.T.C (phenylthiocarbamide) is a learned, not inherited, trait.

questions.question: Which of the following statements is correct about the ABO blood group system?

options.A: Antigens are located on the surface of white blood cells
options.B: Antibodies are located in the blood plasma
options.C: Antibodies are located on the surface of the red blood cells
options.D: Antigens are located in the blood plasma

questions.explanation: Antibodies circulate in plasma, while antigens are on red blood cell surfaces.

questions.question: A woman with blood group A gives birth to a child with blood group O. Which of the following blood groups cannot belong to the father?
questions.explanation: A child with blood group O must inherit O alleles; father cannot be AB.

questions.question: The diagram above illustrates a genetic cross between two parents. The parental gametes I, II, III and IV gave rise to the offspring below. Study it and answer questions 43 and 44.
questions.explanation: The gametes show heterozygous and homozygous combinations.

questions.question: Which of the following statements is correct about the cross?
questions.explanation: The cross indicates heterozygous parents producing a typical Mendelian ratio.

questions.question: The physical appearance of an organism is described as its
questions.explanation: Phenotype refers to observable traits.

questions.question: Mutation leads to evolution because it

options.A: kills all organisms where it has occurred
options.B: gives rise to offspring having same characteristics as the parents
options.C: does not confer adaptability to the offspring
options.D: gives rise to new individuals that differ from their parents

questions.explanation: Mutations introduce genetic variation, essential for evolution.

questions.question: The male and female gametes which bear genes for inheritance in plants are located in the
questions.explanation: Stamens produce male gametes; pistils produce female gametes.

questions.question: Which type of breeding is represented by industrial melanism in peppered moths?
questions.explanation: Industrial melanism is an example of natural selection favoring dark moths in polluted environments.

questions.question: In a matching exercise, a student paired scientists with their famous work. The scientists that are not correctly paired are
questions.explanation: Charles Darwin is known for evolution, Mendel for inheritance, Lamarck for acquired traits, Pasteur for germ theory.

questions.question: Which of the scientists were involved in the study of evolution?
questions.explanation: Charles Darwin and Lamarck contributed significantly to evolutionary theory.
